evidence_summary: Graph neural networks can utilize graph structure to enhance node classification accuracy in scenarios with scarce labeled nodes by encoding the graph structure directly using a neural network model.
A scalable approach for semi-supervised learning on graph-structured data is based on an efficient variant of convolutional neural networks that operate directly on graphs.

The model learns hidden layer representations that encode both local graph structure and features of nodes, and outperforms related methods by a significant margin in experiments on citation networks and a knowledge graph dataset.
